assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan references inspecting multiple existing files: (1) file-5-description.md explicitly states 'should be confirmed by reading the actual get.rs sibling file'; plan.md line 46 says 'following get.rs pattern'; (2) plan.md file 2 says 'following existing method signatures' for advisory/service/advisory.rs; (3) plan.md Cross-Section Reference Consistency references 'AdvisorySummary' at 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs' and 'AppError' at 'common/src/error.rs'. The conventions.md analysis also identifies patterns from these files (e.g., 'AppError enum defined in common/src/error.rs'). At least 3 of the 4 listed files are referenced for inspection/analysis.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan lists exactly 6 files across Files to Modify and Files to Create: (1) advisory/model/mod.rs (MODIFY), (2) advisory/service/advisory.rs (MODIFY), (3) advisory/endpoints/mod.rs (MODIFY), (4) advisory/model/severity_summary.rs (CREATE), (5) advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs (CREATE), (6) tests/api/advisory_summary.rs (CREATE). No other files are listed for modification or creation in plan.md or any file-description file. The file descriptions (file-1 through file-6) correspond 1:1 to these planned files.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes creating the 3 new files listed in Files to Create: severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint handler, and advisory_summary.rs test file
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Files to Create section lists: (4) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/severity_summary.rs' (model), (5) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs' (endpoint handler), (6) 'tests/api/advisory_summary.rs' (test file). All three are marked as CREATE with detailed descriptions in file-4-description.md, file-5-description.md, and file-6-description.md respectively.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes modifying the 3 files listed in Files to Modify: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, and advisory/model/mod.rs
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Files to Modify section lists: (1) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/mod.rs' (MODIFY), (2) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs' (MODIFY), (3) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/mod.rs' (MODIFY). All three are marked as MODIFY with detailed descriptions in file-1-description.md, file-2-description.md, and file-3-description.md respectively.

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 83-91 show the commit message: 'feat(api): add advisory severity aggregation endpoint' which follows Conventional Commits format (type=feat, scope=api, description). The footer contains 'Implements TC-9201' referencing the ticket number.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: Searched all output files (plan.md, conventions.md, file-1 through file-6-description.md) for 'trailer', 'Assisted-by', or 'Claude Code'. None of these terms appear anywhere in the outputs. The commit message in plan.md (lines 82-91) does not include an Assisted-by trailer, and no instruction to use --trailer flag is mentioned.

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: The plan references the Target Branch as 'main' (plan.md lines 7-8: '## Target Branch\nmain'). However, there is no mention anywhere in the outputs of creating a branch named 'TC-9201', no branch naming convention is documented, and no 'git checkout -b' or branch creation step is included. The assertion requires both parts; only the target branch reference is present.

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md clearly identifies both patterns: (1) Error handling — line 17: 'All handlers return Result<T, AppError> with .context() wrapping for error propagation'; lines 36-37: 'AppError enum defined in common/src/error.rs, implements IntoResponse. Errors wrapped with .context("descriptive message") (anyhow-style)'; (2) Module structure — line 6: 'Each domain module follows model/ + service/ + endpoints/ directory structure'; line 7: 'Module registration is done via mod.rs files with pub mod <submodule>; declarations'.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 7-8 contain '## Target Branch' followed by 'main', explicitly identifying the target branch value from the task description.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: Searched all output files for 'digest', 'description digest', 'Step 1.5' (in this context), 'backward compatibility', and 'description-digest-protocol'. None of these terms appear anywhere in the outputs. The plan has no step or mention related to checking for a description digest comment or the backward compatibility behavior.
